title: John Krasinski on 'A Quiet Place' and the Question of a Sequel | Interview (Ep. 103)
format: Sean Fennessey sits down with John Krasinski on writing, directing and starring in A Quiet Place, working opposite Emily Blunt, and why he came around to a sequel.

films.title: A Quiet Place
films.note: His third film as director, pitched as a family drama Trojan-horsed as genre. He rewrote and starred after Emily Blunt read the script on a plane and told him no one else could do it; her bathtub scene was one take.

films.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street
films.note: Saw it around age 12 and 'blacked out for six years,' which is why he avoided horror for decades.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: Cited alongside A Quiet Place as recent genre films doing social commentary; he's a fan of Jordan Peele's.

films.title: The Witch
films.note: The goat 'blacked me out for another six years,' growing up Catholic.

films.title: Let the Right One In
films.note: The original: 'one of the best love stories I've ever seen,' that happens to involve a vampire.

films.title: Jaws
films.note: A touchstone: genre used to explore things, not just to scare.

films.title: Rosemary's Baby
films.note: Touchstone in the same vein as Jaws and Alien.

films.title: Brief Interviews with Hideous Men
films.note: His directing debut, made 'out of sheer ignorance'; cinematographer John Bailey later walked him through what could have gone wrong.

films.title: The Hollars
films.note: His second directing job; he took far longer in prep, refusing to move until he had the exact team and cast.

films.title: Promised Land
films.note: Co-wrote with Matt Damon, who wanted to direct it at the time.

films.title: Manchester by the Sea
films.note: His first idea, pitched to Matt Damon and handed off to Kenneth Lonergan, 'my story,' but he'd never have made it himself.

films.title: Good Will Hunting
films.note: The Boston-tattoo movie; the definition of what he connects to at the movies.

films.title: Kramer vs. Kramer
films.note: The kind of intense family-unit story he wanted A Quiet Place to be underneath the genre.

films.title: Ordinary People
films.note: Named alongside Kramer vs. Kramer as the '70s-drama model for the film.

films.title: Jurassic Park
films.note: ILM's Scott Farrar, who designed the raptor scene, stayed on the effects for seven weeks after Krasinski led with 'I don't know.'

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: Farrar ran the camera for the opening imperial-ship shot on the first film.

films.title: Saving Private Ryan
films.note: On the sound team's résumé (from this to Malick to Avatar) before they begged to go start designing the film.

films.title: Mary Poppins Returns
films.note: The 'tiny indie movie' Emily Blunt was shooting, with a twelve-week-old at home, while he was writing.

films.title: Ant-Man
films.note: What he went back to watching on the plane while Blunt read the script.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: His last great thing seen: a '70s movie he fell in love with; he's friendly with Bradley Cooper.

films.title: Roma
films.note: The movie he was most excited to catch up on after four months shooting in Colombia.

films.title: The Verdict
films.note: His favorite movie of all time; he saw a lot of it in A Star Is Born.

films.title: The Firm
films.note: Sean's plug, a personal favorite he broke down on The Rewatchables.
